abridged Sarhhitd are quoted in his Vivrti. That work must have
been lost soon after Utpala's time, since Al-Blruni says nothing
about it. Similar is the case with the work of an ancient author
named Sdrasvata who had written on underground water-sources.
however, quotes only 31 Slokashom the above work, which
would, otherwise, have remained a mere name.
(ix) Utpala quotes, for the benefit of his pupils, 32 verses of
the Akfarakoia of TavaneSvara, occurring in the Bodhiyatrd dealing
with journeys and horary astrology.1 Elsewhere he quotes five verses
of a Tdtrdkdra named Siddhartha.2 It may be conjectured that some
Buddhist saint, a a, had written a work on Tdtrd, which
Tavaneivara might have expounded, adding his own knowledge and
experience.
1. Vide XCVI. 13.
2. „ CIV. 60.
